trade.summary: Brazil exports iron ore, soybeans, crude oil, and copper to South Korea. South Korea exports vehicles, ships, electronics, and machinery to Brazil. South Korea is a major destination for Brazilian minerals.

diplomatic.summary: Growing diplomatic relations. Both countries cooperate in G20 and share interest in free trade and multilateral institutions. Regular bilateral meetings. South Korea has shown interest in Brazilian lithium and other critical minerals.

military.summary: No military alliance. South Korean defense companies have sold armored vehicles and equipment to Brazil. Growing defense industrial relationship.

financial.summary: South Korean companies (Samsung, LG, Hyundai, Posco) have significant investments in Brazil. Growing Korean FDI in Brazilian manufacturing and battery materials sector.

energy.summary: South Korea imports Brazilian crude oil. Korean companies interested in Brazilian LNG and energy projects. Cooperation on offshore oil technology.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation, particularly in materials science, semiconductors, and agricultural technology. Korean government has increased S&T cooperation with Brazil.
